review_summary.college_name: Raipur Institute of Medical Sciences Reviews on Placements, Faculty & Facilities

individual_reviews.course: MBBS - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery - Batch of 2026
individual_reviews.title: Raipur Rims at a glance.

content.Placements: 150 students are admitted in our class. There is no package kind in MBBS. After MBBS, local practice in the government or private sector. Whole do internship for one year compulsory. After the MBBS course, some students start practicing, and some students start their PG in the respective department.
content.Infrastructure: There is a separate hostel & college building. Our hospital has 700 beds. There are two libraries that are fully Wi-Fi and have proper setting facilities. Classrooms are well-equipped with AC & ample setting areas. In a hostel, one room is shared with another student.

individual_reviews.course: MBBS - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery - Batch of 2026
individual_reviews.title: RIMS Raipur college in a nutshell.

content.Placements: 150 students are admitted in my class. There is a nonpackage kind of thing in MBBS. After an internship, everyone can practice medicine in any corporate or government hospital. The whole batch gets to do an internship compulsorily for one year. Some do PG preparation also.
content.Infrastructure: There are two libraries with fully Wi-Fi and proper sitting areas. Classrooms are well-equipped with AC and ample sitting area. In hostels one has to share a room with another guy. The hostel is equipped with 2 beds and 1 almirah with a fan. One can also opt for AC rooms at some expense.

individual_reviews.course: MBBS - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery - Batch of 2023
individual_reviews.title: 75% of students got placement in my course, and my course had a package of Rs 50,000 to Rs 100,000.

content.Placements: 75% of students got placement in my course. My course had a package of Rs 50,000 to Rs 100,000 at Raipur Institute of Medical Science. In my course, 90% of people get internships in their own hospital, RIMS. The best training is given in our hospital.
content.Infrastructure: Our college has about 20 classrooms, 1 lecture room, 1 auditorium, and a boys' hostel with at least 50 rooms, along with a girls' hostel that also has 50 rooms. There is a canteen where the food is clean and neat.
content.Faculty: All the teachers here are very understanding and professional. Yes, it prepares the student for the industry. In this college, the exam is held early in the semester, and the passing number is 100/50. I feel that our college is the best in Raipur because, along with your superspecialist hospital, you also get the best campus. You are getting internships in your own hospital, so what else is good about it?

individual_reviews.course: MBBS - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery - Batch of 2024
individual_reviews.title: Fantastic college, you will learn a lot more here.

content.Placements: The placement is okay. The package depends on state to state, government, and private hospitals. There is no such meaning as placement in the medical profession. The top role offered just after the undergraduate is a junior resident doctor in AIIMS.
content.Infrastructure: Everything is available there but the infrastructure is not that good. The building is not well painted and is just whitewashed. However, it is okay from the inside. The mess food is okay and the hostel rooms are good. Sports and games are not regularly played.

individual_reviews.course: MBBS - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery - Batch of 2024
individual_reviews.title: Everything is available there. The building is well painted. However it is okay from inside.

content.Placements: The placement is okay. The package depends on state to state, government and private hospitals. There is no as such meaning of placement in medical profession. The top role offered just after the undergraduation is of a junior resident doctor in AIIMS.
content.Infrastructure: Some of the professors are really helpful and knowledgeable. They have helped us a lot. The course and curriculum is relevant and it does make the students industry ready. Good campus with proper lights, play ground. Like cricket, football, badminton and gym also.

reviews_list.review_title: Faculty is excellent but no extracurricular activities is a drawback.

review_content.Placements: All of our seniors who got passed out this year were placed in some or the other companies after their internship. Only a few students cracked the NEET PG exam. Most students were placed into hospitals after their internship. The package is 95,000/- per month which is very reasonable for freshers.
review_content.Infrastructure: Hostels are okay for students with an accommodation for two students. For 1st and 2nd year students, two students share a room but after second year students get a single room. The hostel fee is reasonable that it is just 1800/- (eighteen hundred rupees only) with 10,000 security deposit which will be given back to you when you leave the college.

reviews_list.batch_info: MBBS - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery - Batch of 2025
reviews_list.review_title: ESIC- A plethora of knowledge and creativity.

review_content.Placements: After MBBS you have to attend the internship period, one doesn't have to worry much about internships. Almost all the students got to work for the super specialty and various other departments of our college. The stipend is usually15k-20k, best among other state-based MBBS colleges.
review_content.Infrastructure: About hostels: For girls:- We've a well furnished 2 bhk flats given for 4 people. A store, kitchen, hall is there along with 2 washrooms. For boys:- They have 2 people in a considerably big room. They share common washrooms. There is another boys hostel which is being renovated. About college: we've a 250 seater lecture hall-cum-auditorium. Additionally, each department has a 50 seater, 120 seater, departmental library and a reading room. We've a central library connected with a reading room. Entire campus is well air conditioned. Each department has its own museum and labs connected to it.

reviews_list.batch_info: MBBS - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery - Batch of 2025
reviews_list.review_title: It is an amazing emerging college in the south of India.

review_content.Placements: Since this is a medical college, right after 4 years of your MBBS course, you have to do a 1-year internship at your college only, and during this time you also get a stipend. After the completion, you can either serve a bond of 1 year as a doctor in the college or pay 5 lacks and break the bond.

content.Infrastructure: The college is well-equipped with modern facilities such as a college library, laboratories and lecture halls. The hostel is separated for boys and girls. There is a main boys hostel. Esic is a central college. So funding and infrastructure are very good. There is one mess and one canteen.

individual_reviews.course: MBBS - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery - Batch of 2025
individual_reviews.title: Our college provides great infrastructure and teachers.

content.Placements: It is a good college that provides good placement offers. It is a central cum state government college, so there are many benefits. Students who complete MBBS in this college have to submit a bond of working here for one year or pay Rs.500000 for canceling the bond.
content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure of our college is the best for MBBS in Hyderabad with a clean and calm environment. It is in the center of Hyderabad with a metro facility. Labs are good but Wi-Fi is not available. Library is very good with friendly people. Hostel rooms are good. Our college has central air-conditioners.

individual_reviews.course: MBBS - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery - Batch of 2025
individual_reviews.title: Faculty is excellent but no extracurricular activities is a drawback.

content.Placements: All of our seniors who got passed out this year were placed in some or the other companies after their internship. Only a few students cracked the NEET PG exam. Most students were placed into hospitals after their internship. The package is 95,000/- per month which is very reasonable for freshers.
content.Infrastructure: Hostels are okay for students with an accommodation for two students. For 1st and 2nd year students, two students share a room but after second year students get a single room. The hostel fee is reasonable that it is just 1800/- (eighteen hundred rupees only) with 10,000 security deposit which will be given back to you when you leave the college.

individual_reviews.course: MBBS - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery - Batch of 2025
individual_reviews.title: ESIC- A plethora of knowledge and creativity.

content.Placements: After MBBS you have to attend the internship period, one doesn't have to worry much about internships. Almost all the students got to work for the super specialty and various other departments of our college. The stipend is usually15k-20k, best among other state-based MBBS colleges.
content.Infrastructure: About hostels: For girls:- We've a well furnished 2 bhk flats given for 4 people. A store, kitchen, hall is there along with 2 washrooms. For boys:- They have 2 people in a considerably big room. They share common washrooms. There is another boys hostel which is being renovated. About college: we've a 250 seater lecture hall-cum-auditorium. Additionally, each department has a 50 seater, 120 seater, departmental library and a reading room. We've a central library connected with a reading room. Entire campus is well air conditioned. Each department has its own museum and labs connected to it.

individual_reviews.course: MBBS - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery - Batch of 2025
individual_reviews.title: It is an amazing emerging college in the south of India.

content.Placements: Since this is a medical college, right after 4 years of your MBBS course, you have to do a 1-year internship at your college only, and during this time you also get a stipend. After the completion, you can either serve a bond of 1 year as a doctor in the college or pay 5 lacks and break the bond.
content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure of ESIC medical college Hyderabad is one of its main highlights. We have well-equipped labs, a clean campus, and fully functional hostels also available. The hostels have 2 sharing for the first 2 years after which single rooms are allotted. Although there is no Wi-Fi provided by the college, you can install your own. The mess is rather average at best with a fee of Rs. 3,675 per month.

For round 4, the closing cutoff for the General AI category ranged from 25203 to 47704. According to this range, the most and least competitive courses are MS and MD, respectively. table.details: Sree Balaji Medical College and Hospital (SBMCH)

college_info.intro: Balaji Medical College offers UG and PG-level programmes under Pre-Clinical, Para-Clinical, Clinical and Super Speciality categories. The Institute offers MBBS, MD and MS as popular programmes. SBMCH offers these programmes in various specialisations for the duration of 66 months to 3 years. Candidates pursuing for MBBS must qualify Class 12 with a minimum aggregate of 50% and Physics, Biology, Chemistry and English as primary subjects. The remaining programmes such as DM, MD and MS consider NEET UG/ NEET SS/ NEET PG qualification scores/percentile to pursue one. Furthermore, candidates must have an MBBS degree with 50% aggregate obtained from an institute recognised by the Medical Council of India to seek admission in MD and MS programmes. The fee structure of all these programmes ranges somewhere around INR 10 Lakh to INR 2.1 Cr. To learn more about Balaji Medical College popular programmes, check out the table given below:

faqs.answer: A:    There is no doubt that everyone who graduates from this reputable college will find a solid career at multispecialty hospitals because of the college's reputation. We all understand how crucial a surgeon's position is in the current situation of healthcare, therefore stipends are highly alluring.Students at this college find jobs much faster than at other colleges because it is also connected to a hospital. However, this won't happen until one year of internship is over, or when the MBBS p
faqs.question: Is Sree Balaji Medical College and Hospital, Chennai good for placements?

faqs.answer: A:    Students can pursue further studies (PG in MS/MD) after pursuing MBBS. Students can also work as general practitioner in a hospital. They can also start their own clinic after pursuing MBBS from SBMCH . To know more about admission in SBMCH, refer to the official website of the college.
faqs.question: What are the career prospects after completing MBBS from SBMCH?

faqs.answer: A:    Yes, VIMSAR Burla and Hindu Mission Hospital be considered as some of the top recruiters of SBMCH among many others. SBMCH offers placement record of 90-100 percent students. To know more about admissions in SBMCH, Refer to the official website of the college and enquire about the same.
faqs.question: Can VIMSAR Burla and Hindu Mission Hospital be considered as some of the top recruiters of SBMCH?

fees_and_eligibility.overview: Sree Balaji Medical College and Hospital course admissions are conducted on the basis of merit and entrance. The Institute offers UG and PG level programmes in Pre-Clinical, Para- Clinical, Clinical and Super Speciality. SBMCH offers programmes such as MBBS, MD, MS and BPharma and their specialisations such as Radiodiagnosis, Paediatrics, Obstetrics and Gynaecology, Dermatology, Venereology and Leprosy, General Medicine, ENT, Ophthalmology etc. Balaji Medical College fee structure ranges between INR 10 Lakh to INR 2.1 Cr. To be eligible for MBBS, candidates must secure an aggregate of in with (Physics, Chemistry, Biology) PCB as primary subjects. Whereas, for courses such as MD and MS, candidates must have an MBBS degree with aggregate. Furthermore, candidates must have qualified NEET UG / NEET PG/ NEET SS for the respective chosen programme. To learn more about Balaji Medical College courses, eligibility and fees, check out the table given below:

faqs.answer: A:    Students must appear and qualify the entrance examinations to ensure their admission in SBMCH . However, certain seats are reserved for the management quota, Students can call in the office of SBMCH to enquire about the same. To know more , visit the official website of SBMCH.
faqs.question: Can students join SBMCH directly without any entrance exam?

faqs.answer: A:    Admission to MBBS is based on the score of NEET UG in every college and candidates should have PCB in Class 12 grade.Yes, one can get admission in SBMCH with 60 % in Class 12 as the minimum eligibility criteria for students is 50% aggregate in Class 12 but, it is also necessary to give NEET UG and then the admission is given based on score and merit list released by the college. Candidates can take admission by searching for the merit list on the college portal followed by counselling. Candidates can submit the fees for securing a seat in the college.
faqs.question: Can I get admission to SBMCH for MBBS with 60% in Class 12th?

faqs.answer: A:    For admission to MS at Sree Balaji Medical College and Hospital,  candidates must have an MBBS degree with at least 50% aggregate from an institute recognised by Medical Council of India. Selection is done on the basis of score obtained in NEET Pg entrance exam.
faqs.question: What is the eligibility criteria for MS at Sree Balaji Medical College and Hospital Chennai?

faqs.answer: A:    Sree Balaji Medical College and Hospital offers MS programme at PG level.  There are four specialisations available which are Orthopaedics, General Surgery, ENT, Ophthalmology. The seats intake for MS programme is 42. Candidates must note that this information is sourced from the official website of the institute/university sanctioning body and is subject to change.
faqs.question: How many students can do MS at SBMCH Chennai?

reviews_list.batch_info: Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery (MBBS) - Batch of 2029
reviews_list.review_title: Honest review about the college.

review_content.Placements: After MBBS there won't be many job opportunities because a postgraduate degree is a must in order to practice a good medical career in India. But if we take an average number the salary of a plan MBBS, doctor ranges from Rs. 40,000 to Rs. 60,000 depending upon the state and the city you are practicing in.
review_content.Infrastructure: Classrooms of our college are air-conditioned and well-maintained. Campus infrastructure is well-built and has all facilities. Hostel is both air-conditioned and non-air-conditioned. Air-conditioned hostel has just been started and the food in air-conditioned hostel is better compared to non-air-conditioned hostel. There is one ground, one indoor sports complex, turf, and a basketball court for students to play.

reviews_list.batch_info: Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery (MBBS) - Batch of 2029
reviews_list.review_title: Surprised by the Quality of Teaching. Improving Infrastructure and Facilities.

review_content.Placements: In MBBS, one has to undergo 4.5 years of study followed by 1 year of internship. Here, the stipend for internship varies from 10,000 to 20,000. Many scholarship programs are happening every now and then. About 60 to 70% of the students here are placed.
review_content.Infrastructure: The facilities provided by the college are average, although they are improving most of the things now. The lecture halls and departments are well maintained, and labs are being upgraded over time. Wi-Fi is free for the students in the library as well as in the lecture halls. Medical consultation for the students is all free except for a few medications. The facilities in the hostel are average; the current hostel buildings are a bit old, but the new hostels are almost ready for use. The food served in the hostel is good enough and hygienic.

review_summary.college_name: Balaji Medical College Reviews on Placements, Faculty & Facilities

individual_reviews.course: MS in General Surgery - Batch of 2026
individual_reviews.title: A good college gives you good knowledge.

content.Placements: It's a good college, with good placements, but the nursing staff are very rude to the students who are coming for training and internships.
content.Infrastructure: The hostel is nice, and the food is better than last year's. We are not allowed to go out after 8 PM, as the warden is strict. We have nice food, with good eggs, and good food, and a good warden. There is a nice climate, with full of trees and plants, surrounded. There is also a nice hospital facilities.
content.Faculty: There are good questions and a good exam hall. We need a hall ticket for exams, and very strict staff, and copying or any malpractice is forbidden. We cannot continue to write exams if we don't follow the rules. We are not allowed to write exams, and the college will be strictly punished. There is a nice faculty, and nice day workers and doctors. Students have opportunities to attend conferences to gain knowledge.

individual_reviews.course: Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery (MBBS) - Batch of 2029
individual_reviews.title: Good teaching faculty but lacks in non-curricular infrastructure.

content.Placements: There is no placement given; even if you pass from any medical college in India you need to have a postgraduate degree so as to get a good job in India but if you want to get a job after planning MBBS. The average salary ranges from 40000 to 60000 and there are some hospitals where you can get those jobs.
content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure of the college is great with modern classrooms, labs, library and good Wi-Fi. The sports facility and old hostel are very poor. The old hostels are very poorly made and the food is very repetitive and bad with no variety. Few sports courts are poorly maintained and always occupied and don't meet the requirements.

individual_reviews.course: Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery (MBBS) - Batch of 2029
individual_reviews.title: Honest review about the college.

content.Placements: After MBBS there won't be many job opportunities because a postgraduate degree is a must in order to practice a good medical career in India. But if we take an average number the salary of a plan MBBS, doctor ranges from Rs. 40,000 to Rs. 60,000 depending upon the state and the city you are practicing in.
content.Infrastructure: Classrooms of our college are air-conditioned and well-maintained. Campus infrastructure is well-built and has all facilities. Hostel is both air-conditioned and non-air-conditioned. Air-conditioned hostel has just been started and the food in air-conditioned hostel is better compared to non-air-conditioned hostel. There is one ground, one indoor sports complex, turf, and a basketball court for students to play.

individual_reviews.course: Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery (MBBS) - Batch of 2029
individual_reviews.title: Surprised by the Quality of Teaching. Improving Infrastructure and Facilities.

content.Placements: In MBBS, one has to undergo 4.5 years of study followed by 1 year of internship. Here, the stipend for internship varies from 10,000 to 20,000. Many scholarship programs are happening every now and then. About 60 to 70% of the students here are placed.
content.Infrastructure: The facilities provided by the college are average, although they are improving most of the things now. The lecture halls and departments are well maintained, and labs are being upgraded over time. Wi-Fi is free for the students in the library as well as in the lecture halls. Medical consultation for the students is all free except for a few medications. The facilities in the hostel are average; the current hostel buildings are a bit old, but the new hostels are almost ready for use. The food served in the hostel is good enough and hygienic.

individual_reviews.course: Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery (MBBS) - Batch of 2022
individual_reviews.title: The college has a very good faculty, average infrastructure but a confident setup.

content.Placements: The education you get from this institution is very good just like in any other private medical hospital. But only if the student has thirst for knowledge and has an inquisitive mind. Only when he/she asks doubts and shows interest, faculty members will be ready to engage with them and share their valuable experience and knowledge with the students. Many of them in senior batches have got decent and above-average scores and have cleared NEET PG with ease and have been placed in government colleges for their post-graduate studies.
content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure compared to other private medical colleges is just average. It's not so great to boast about. But the infrastructure is just enough to give us what a basic medical college will have to offer. We do have hi speed internet connection in the college as well as the hospital. The library is very well-equipped with amazing books and the classrooms are well ventilated and air-conditioned.

admission_overview.college_name: Balaji Medical College Admissions 2025: Fees, Eligibility, Dates & Selection Criteria

admission_process.title: SBMCH Admission Process 2025

paragraphs: SBMCH admission process 2025isentrance-basedfor all the programmes.Sree Balaji Medical College and Hospital(SBMCH) offers various UG, PG, and post-doctoral programmes. At the UG level, it provides anMBBS programme,and admission is solely based on theNEET UGscore. Interested candidates can download the application form from the official website ofSBMCH Chennaiand send it by post, or apply online.

Sree Balaji Medical College, Chennai is thebest teaching hospitalthat provides all levels of healthcare facilities including majorsuper-specialty servicesand offersexcellent trainingopportunities to the students. SBMCH admission to UG, PG and postdoctoral courses is entrance-based. Check out below the details on eligibility and selection criteria forSBM College courses:
Class 12 from a recognised board withPhysics,Chemistry,Biology& English individually, and at least 50% aggregate taken together in PCB,
Candidate must be at least17 years old
MD inMedicineor MD in Paediatrics
MBBS degree with at least 50% aggregate from an institute recognised by the Medical Council of India
MBBS degree with at least 50% aggregate from an institute recognised by the Medical Council of India
NOTE:In the absence of an official update, the above information has been sourced from non-official websites and, is therefore indicative.
DownloadMBBS_Course_Detail
Sree Balaji Medical College and Hospital fees for different courses lie betweenINR 2 lakhandINR 1.3 Cr.

Affordability is one of the main reasons why so many students drop out of college. Although the availability of education loans and scholarships has made students' academic journeys easier, but meeting the required eligibility criteria for aneducation loanor a scholarship often stands as a challenge.
Find below theSBMCH tuition feealong with average package details for the popular courses:
Tuition Fee (Investment)
Average Package (Return)
Note:Candidates must note that this information is sourced from the official website of the institute/university-sanctioning body and is subject to change.

SBMCH has an annual intake of 250 admissions per year for the UG programme in medicine (MBBS), and163 seats(MD/MS/DM) per year in various PG and Super speciality programmes. The seat reservation for respective categories shall be as per applicable laws prevailing in States/Union Territories. Find below the course-wise seat intake at Sree Balaji Medical College and Hospital:
Seat Intake
Note:Candidates must note that this information is sourced from the official website of the institute/university-sanctioning body and is subject to change.

For the MBBS programme, 3% of seats of the annual intake will be allotted to candidates with disability of lower limbs between 50% to 70%. In case any seat in this 3% quota remains vacant, then any such unfilled seat will be filled up by persons with locomotor disability of lower limbs between 40% to 50%.

Candidates may still take the NEET exam even if the qualifying examination results have not been released. In case of selection, candidates shall not be admitted to the SBM College course until they fulfil the eligibility criteria. Below mentioned is the step-by-step admission process for Sree Balaji Medical College and Hospital:
Step 1: Application
Candidates must apply on the official website of NEET (exams.nta.ac.in/NEET)
Step 2: Appear for Entrance Exam
To be eligible for MBBS admission, candidates must obtain a minimum50 percentilein the NEET exam.
Step 3: Selection round: Merit-list
An all-India merit list and state-wise merit list of the eligible candidates will be prepared on the basis of the score obtained in NEET.
Step 4: Counselling & Seats Allotment
Seats will be allotted as per the merit andchoice filledfor the college during thecounselling
Step 5: Reporting
Candidates must report to the college and pay the course fee to confirm the admission.

faqs.question: Can I get admission to SBMCH for MBBS with 60% in Class 12th?
faqs.answer: Admission to MBBS is based on the score of NEET UG in every college and candidates should have PCB in Class 12 grade.Yes, one can get admission in SBMCH with60 % in Class 12as the minimum eligibility criteria for students is 50% aggregate in Class 12 but, it is also necessary to giveNEET UGand then the admission is given based on score andmerit listreleased by the college. Candidates can take admission by searching for the merit list on the college portal followed bycounselling. Candidates can s

faqs.question: What is Sree Balaji Medical College and Hospital Cutoff 2023?
faqs.answer: Sree Balaji Medical College and Hospital Cutoff 2023was concluded after Round 6 for admission to MBBS courses. Talking about the last three years' cutoff, Sree Balaji Medical College and Hospital NEET cutoff 2023 for Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery (MBBS) closed at1221896while in 2022, it closed at846522and918922in 2021. Also, for Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery (MBBS), Sree Balaji Medical College and Hospital has witnessed a fluctuating trend over the years for the Gen

faqs.question: Can students with minimum cutoff get a seat at SBMCH?
faqs.answer: Students with an All India Rank of 4 Lakhs can easily get admission to Sree Balaji Medical College and Hospital (SBMCH) in Chennai. The minimum eligibility for MBBS is 10+2 in PCB with at least 50% marks. The closing ranking for MBBS at SBMCH in 2022 was 81,387. 2023 cut off score for MBBS for general and EWS category is 720 - 137.The overall cutoff for NEET is 1218682 rank.

faqs.question: Can students join SBMCH directly without any entrance exam?
faqs.answer: Students must appear and qualify the entrance examinations to ensure their admission in SBMCH . However, certain seats are reserved for the management quota, Students can call in the office of SBMCH to enquire about the same. To know more , visit the official website of SBMCH.

faqs.question: Are admissions at SBMCH entrance-based or merit-based?
faqs.answer: Admission in SBMCH is done solely on entrance basis. Students must appear and qualify the entrance examinations to secure their seats in their desired courses at SBMCH.To know more regarding admission in SBMCH,  students can go through the official website of the college.

faqs.question: Does SBMCH accept NEET score?
faqs.answer: Yes , SBMCH accepts admission through national level entrance examination - NEET UG , students have to score at least minimum cutoff to get admission in the college. To know more regarding the admission process in SBMCH, students can go through the official website of SBMCH and enquire about it .

faqs.question: How do students get selected for MBBS at SBMCH and what is the minimum score required?
faqs.answer: Students are selected for the MBBS programme at Sree Balaji Medical College and Hospital (SBMCH) in Chennai based on thier NEET UG scores. The Directorate General of Health Sciences in New Delhi conducts the counseling.The minimum eligibility criteria for MBBS at SBMCH are:Age: 17 years as of December 31 of the admission year10+2: 50% marks in PCB with English as the main subjectNEET: RequiredThe minimum score required for each subject is to attend 80% of the total number of classes.The admission process for MBBS at SBMCH is:Qualify for the NEET UG entrance examParticipate in the Tamil Nadu NEET UG CounsellingSeat allotmentDocument verificationFee payment

faqs: What is Sree Balaji Medical College and Hospital Cutoff 2023?
description: Sree Balaji Medical College and Hospital cutoff 2025 is available on this page for various accepting exams. Candidates can refer to the exam-wise sections below to view the cutoff details across different rounds and categories. Other important updates are as follows:

Sree Balaji Medical College and Hospital NEET UG cutoff 2025 was concluded with the release of the final seat allotment result for admission to the MBBS course. The cutoff was released for several categories belonging to the AI quota. For the General AI quota, the NEET UG last round closing rank was 791775 for MBBS admission. Candidates can refer to the table below to view the first and last round cutoffs 2025 for the General AI category students.
Sree Balaji Medical College and Hospital MBBS cutoff 2025 was 791775 for the students belonging to the General AI quota. For other categories and rounds, use the provided filters.

Sree Balaji Medical College and Hospital accepts the NEET UG entrance exam for selecting students to the MBBS course. Candidates with a valid score are then eligible for the NEET UG counselling. For 2026 admission, aspirants must refer to the table below to view some of the important NEET UG 2026 dates to be eligible for admission.

Candidates preparing for admission can refer to the table below to view or download the NEET UG round and year-wise consolidated cutoff list. This will help students to understand the past three years' cutoff trends.

Sree Balaji Medical College and Hospital NEET PG cutoff 2025 has been released for admission to various PG-level courses, like MD and MS. The cutoff was released in the form of opening and closing ranks for multiple categories under the All India quota. For round 4, the closing cutoff for the General AI category ranged from 103375 to 225619. According to this range, the most competitive course is MD. For more details, see the table below.

According to the Sree Balaji Medical College and Hospital MD cutoff 2025, the most sought-after specialisations for the General AI category were MD in General Medicine, followed by MD in Pulmonary Medicine and MD in Pathology. The round 4 closing ranks for these specialisations were 103375, 137324 and 156615, respectively.
Students who appeared for the NEET PG exam 2025 can refer to the table below to view the NEET PG Round 1 cutoff list. They can also download the results for later use.

Sree Balaji Medical College and Hospital NEET SS Cutoff 2024 was released for the concluding round in the form of ranks for admission to DM course across multiple specialisations. The cutoff was released for various categories belonging to the AI quota.

While for the last round, the closing rank was 8242 wherein DM in Neurology proved to be the most sought-after in the General AI category. Students can check the first and last round closing ranks from the table provided below.

In this section, candidates can find out the category-wise NEET SS cutoff 2024 and 2023. In 2024, for the General AI quota, the NEET SS cutoff was closed at 8242 for DM in Neurology. In 2023, the same specialisation cutoff was capped at 8143.
For more in-depth details for the previous year cutoffs, please refer to the table provided below and make use of the filters available to refine your search on the basis of rounds and category.
Students can view or download the round and year-wise consolidated NEET SS cutoff PDFs for Sree Balaji Medical College and Hospital. This will help students to understand the year-wise cutoff trends.

hostel_campus.college_name: Balaji Medical College Infrastructure Details & Reviews

reviews.review_heading: A good college gives you good knowledge.
reviews.review_text: The hostel is nice, and the food is better than last year's. We are not allowed to go out after 8 PM, as the warden is strict. We have nice food, with good eggs, and good food, and a good warden. There is a nice climate, with full of trees and plants, surrounded. There is also a nice hospital facilities.

reviews.review_heading: Good teaching faculty but lacks in non-curricular infrastructure.
reviews.review_text:
The infrastructure of the college is great with modern classrooms, labs, library and good Wi-Fi.
The sports facility and old hostel are very poor. The old hostels are very poorly made and the food is very repetitive and bad with no variety. Few sports courts are poorly maintained and always occupied and don't meet the requirements.

reviews.review_heading: Honest review about the college.
reviews.review_text: Classrooms of our college are air-conditioned and well-maintained. Campus infrastructure is well-built and has all facilities. Hostel is both air-conditioned and non-air-conditioned. Air-conditioned hostel has just been started and the food in air-conditioned hostel is better compared to non-air-conditioned hostel. There is one ground, one indoor sports complex, turf, and a basketball court for students to play.

reviews.review_heading: Surprised by the Quality of Teaching. Improving Infrastructure and Facilities.
reviews.review_text: The facilities provided by the college are average, although they are improving most of the things now. The lecture halls and departments are well maintained, and labs are being upgraded over time. Wi-Fi is free for the students in the library as well as in the lecture halls. Medical consultation for the students is all free except for a few medications. The facilities in the hostel are average; the current hostel buildings are a bit old, but the new hostels are almost ready for use. The food se...

reviews.review_heading: The college has a very good faculty, average infrastructure but a confident setup.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure compared to other private medical colleges is just average. It's not so great to boast about. But the infrastructure is just enough to give us what a basic medical college will have to offer. We do have hi speed internet connection in the college as well as the hospital. The library is very well-equipped with amazing books and the classrooms are well ventilated and air-conditioned.

reviews.review_heading: A poor institute with poor infrastructure, and a ton of money extracted from students.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure is not good enough. Considering the amount, and they charge per year from a student, one would expect the infrastructure available to be top-notch, but that is not the case as there's NIT even enough room available to occupy the interns available currently in the hospital. Most of the equipment present is not in proper working condition with some wards not even having a sphygmomanometer which is shameful.

reviews.review_heading: Our college has good infrastructure and placements.
reviews.review_text: It is a very good college. The college is spread around 200 acers. They have good computer lab facilities. We have 1000 CC labs and many more labs are also available. The library is also good with air-conditioners. It is a good place to learn more things. The college atmosphere is very good with many trees. The college has all the facilities like Wi-Fi and a canteen.

reviews.review_heading: Satisfied with my college and I'm happy About it.
reviews.review_text:
Infrastructure of the hostel is good. The 
faculty members are well experienced. The 
faculty members are graduated from the best medical college and are placed as professors in my college. 
Food in mess is really good. 
Even other state students and NRI students are comfortable with the mess food.

reviews.review_heading: I'm satisfied with the flow of patients in this college.
reviews.review_text: Classrooms are well organised with proper air-conditioners. Wi-Fi is available in the hospital and college. Hostel facilities are not good and it's better to stay outside. Hospital facilities are average. Hostel management demands a caution deposit which is of Rs.50,000 and an additional amount of Rs.50,000. I recommend students not to take admission to the hostel.

reviews.review_heading: Review of Sree Balaji Medical College and Hospital.
reviews.review_text: Our college provides us with good facilities. The hostel facilities are good. The food served in the hostel is good. Our college has a canteen. It is hard to live in the hostel because the weather in Chennai is changed.

reviews.review_heading: Good infrastructure and facilities. The placements for intern is good.
reviews.review_text: The labs, classrooms, and library are pretty good. In the hostel, the food is slightly not okay, otherwise, all the facilities are good. There are irregular power cuts sometimes and the hostel is cleaned daily by the workers. On weekly basis, mosquito repellent is sprayed once.

reviews.review_heading: It is absolutely good with good placements every year, and good infrastructure and facilities.
reviews.review_text: The faculty members are very good and experienced. They have good knowledge and skills. They are very friendly and thoughtful. Wi-Fi is available in the department. Labs are well-equipped. The library is average. The food is not much good.

reviews.review_heading: Absolutely satisfied because of good placement and really good infrastructure and facilities.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure of the college was good. The parking facility was also there. In college, library maintenance was good. It has nearly 100 books of different authors. The medical facilities were good. The canteen and mess were available. The mess fee should be paid.
